subtitle: The Magic Bullet
summary: Ehrlich and his Japanese assistant Shiga begin a massive trial-and-error hunt, testing nearly five hundred dyes on mice infected with trypanosomes. After countless failures, Ehrlich has the idea to chemically modify a benzopurpurin dye by adding sulfo-groups, producing a new compound called Trypan Red. One mouse is dramatically cured, the first ever to survive trypanosomes, but the results prove inconsistent and the microbes adapt. Despite a thousand disappointments, Ehrlich's growing fame in Frankfurt brings wealthy patrons and a new research institute, the Georg Speyer House, which will fuel his next great experiments.

segments.anchor_sentence: So Paul Ehrlich, in 1902, set out on his hunt.

visual.scene_description: Ehrlich and Shiga in a cluttered laboratory surrounded by hundreds of colorful dye bottles, injecting mice and examining tail blood under a microscope, with every attempt failing.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, cluttered laboratory with shelves of hundreds of colorful dye bottles in mosaic pattern, Paul Ehrlich with messy desk and ever-present cigar and dye-stained coat squatting before open cupboard, Japanese assistant Shiga in white coat snipping tail of white mouse, compound brass microscope on bench, rows of mouse cages, scattered test tubes of blue and yellow liquids

segments.anchor_sentence: Ehrlich was testing the pretty-colored but complicated benzopurpurin dyes on dying mice, but the mice were dying, with sickening regularity, from the mal de Caderas.

visual.scene_description: Ehrlich excitedly waves a test tube of modified red dye at a colleague, having just had his idea to add sulfo-groups, while chemical apparatus and dye bottles crowd every surface around him.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, Paul Ehrlich with dye-stained coat and cigar waving a test tube of bright red liquid at startled colleague, corrugated forehead furrowed in thought, small laboratory crammed with glass flasks and test tubes of colored compounds, chemical apparatus on wooden bench, scattered postal cards with scrawled notes, window showing factory smokestacks in distance

segments.anchor_sentence: Under the skin of two white mice Shiga shot the evil trypanosomes of the mal de Caderas.

visual.scene_description: The dramatic cure: one white mouse with pink-red ears and open eyes stands healthy beside its dead companion, while a circular inset shows trypanosomes vanishing from the blood.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, two white mice in wood-shaving cage, one mouse alert with red-tinted ears and bright open eyes sniffing at its limp dead companion, Ehrlich gesticulating excitedly behind cage with cigar in hand, Shiga holding syringe of red dye, circular microscope inset showing elongated trypanosomes with undulating membranes and flagella dissolving and disappearing from blood

segments.anchor_sentence: Paul Ehrlich, by the grace of persistence, chance, God, and a dye called "Trypan Red" (its real chemical name would stretch across this page!) has saved him!

visual.scene_description: Ehrlich and Shiga look frustrated examining mice that have relapsed, with a circular inset showing resistant trypanosomes thriving despite the red dye surrounding them.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, Paul Ehrlich with dye-stained coat slumped at desk strewn with notes and cigar ash, Shiga snipping mouse tail with scissors over glass slide, rows of mouse cages some with sick mice eyes shut and fur raised, circular microscope inset showing hardy elongated trypanosomes with flagella swarming densely among red blood cells ignoring pink-red dye

segments.anchor_sentence: All the time the laboratory was growing.

visual.scene_description: Ehrlich strides through the grand new Georg Speyer House laboratory, popping into rooms full of chemists and microbe hunters, his voice echoing down the corridors.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, Paul Ehrlich with dye-stained coat and cigar striding down long corridor of grand Georg Speyer House laboratory, multiple open doorways showing chemists at benches with glass flasks and compound brass microscopes, rows of mouse cages, shelves of colorful dye bottles, tall arched windows with natural light, organ grinder visible in garden outside
